title: Content Funding
description: Seed statements for noninflammatory content funding and cross-partisan communication.
notes: Keep the statements phrased as positions real people would sign rather than abstract moderation slogans.
These support the noninflammatory-content pattern and the content-funding surface.

groups.title: Civility topic
notes: This statement is the deployment-time topic statement for noninflammatory/civility alignment attestations. Its uploaded CID should become ALIGNMENT_TOPIC_STATEMENT_CID for the attester and beat-agent policy.

statements.text: I want more political content that helps people understand perspectives they disagree with without contempt, caricature, or inflammatory framing.

groups.title: Right-to-left translation
notes: Moderate-left, moderate-right, and commonality statements for communicating right-leaning ideas without triggering left-leaning readers.

statements.text: I lean left, and I'm interested in reading content that communicates right-wing perspectives as long as it doesn't piss me off.
I lean right, and I'd like to try to communicate right-wing perspectives to common-sense left-leaning people in a way that they'll actually hear.
I'm interested in content that communicates right-wing perspectives in a way that won't piss off left-wingers.

groups.title: Left-to-right translation
notes: Mirror image of the right-to-left seed cluster.

statements.text: I lean right, and I'm interested in reading content that communicates left-wing perspectives as long as it doesn't piss me off.
I lean left, and I'd like to try to communicate left-wing perspectives to common-sense right-leaning people in a way that they'll actually hear.
I'm interested in content that communicates left-wing perspectives in a way that won't piss off right-wingers.

groups.title: Cross-partisan explanatory content
notes: General-purpose statements that can act as parents for both directional translation clusters.

statements.text: I'm interested in content that explains the other side's perspectives, as long as it's written in a way that won't piss me off.
